what_it_does: A standalone HTML preview card specimen for the body typeface (DM Sans): shows large/medium/small text samples, an uppercase+lowercase+numeral glyph set, and a metadata strip listing weights, optical sizing, and usage.
observations: Carries a @dsCard annotation: group='Brand', name='Body — DM Sans', subtitle='UI default with glyph specimen', viewport='700x270'.
Links shared _card.css; local <style> uses tokens var(--font-body), --fg-primary/secondary/muted.
Two-column layout: left = three text specimens (22/16/13px), right = glyph rows A-Z, a-z, digits + & @ ↗.
Sample copy is company-specific: 'Operationalising multi-stakeholder projects', 'novel theoretical model of how all systems work, developed internally over ten years', and a conceptv.io URL preview.
Meta strip declares weights 400·500·600·700, 'Optical sizing 9-40', 'UI default · everywhere body'.
standouts: Specimen copy is drawn from ConceptV's actual positioning (ten-year theoretical model, multi-stakeholder projects, conceptv.io domain) rather than lorem ipsum.
Declares DM Sans as the universal body/UI default.
conventions: Follows the @dsCard gallery convention and var()-token discipline.
Font sizes/weights are hardcoded px/numeric in local CSS rather than referencing type-scale tokens — illustrative specimen values.
notes: DM Sans body-type specimen card; shows text at three sizes, full glyph set, and weight/optical-sizing/usage metadata, using real ConceptV copy.
questions: Are the four weights and 9-40 optical sizing range actually wired into the font loading, or only asserted in this card's metadata?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the @dsCard names it the DM Sans body specimen and that is exactly what renders.
intention_signals: Intended as a recognisable specimen so the body typeface, its weights, and intended usage can be judged by eye in the gallery.
novelty: Using authentic company positioning text as the specimen copy, embedding brand voice into the type sample.
contribution: Contributes the canonical body-typeface specimen (DM Sans) with its weight set, optical-sizing range, glyph coverage, and 'UI default everywhere body' role declaration.
summary_for_embedding: An HTML @dsCard preview specimen for DM Sans as the design system's body/UI typeface, showing sized samples, a glyph set, and weight/optical-sizing metadata.
intention_for_embedding: To give an eye-recognisable reference for the body typeface, its weights, and its role as the universal UI default.
